title: lathyrus latifolius var. algericus
intro: Lathyrus latifolius var. algericus, also known as Algerian pease or broad-leaved pease, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and astringent, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including urinary tract infections and digestive problems. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving circulation, as well as having antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Lathyrus latifolius var. algericus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be used as a component in topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
